evidence.q1: Sections numbered sequentially 1 through 15; no internal cross-references, so no resolution failures.
evidence.q2: Defined terms 'Personal Data', 'Processing', 'Personal Data Breach' are defined once in Section 2 and used consistently throughout.
evidence.q3: All time periods are specific and commercial: 'within 2 business days', '24 hours', '10 business days', '30 days', '90 days', 'three months', etc. No vague phrasing.
evidence.q4: Document uses modern 'will' throughout and contains no archaic or out-of-register legal diction like 'shall', 'herein', or 'hereto'.
evidence.q5: Every sentence is grammatically correct and none is internally contradictory.
evidence.q6: Clause phrasing register is uniform: imperative 'Vendor will' / 'Customer will' style maintained in all sections, no authorial shift.
